steps.title: Recognize the subject-verb boundary
steps.content:
Before the blank: “Her portrait of novelist Zadie Smith” = complete subject noun phrase
After the blank: “is displayed in the National Portrait Gallery in London” = verb phrase beginning the predicate

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A subject and its verb should not be separated by punctuation in a simple sentence. D correctly omits punctuation, allowing the subject to flow directly to the verb. A, B, and C each introduce unnecessary punctuation (em dash, colon, comma). This matches choice D — D is correct.
